reason: Modifying /etc/fstab to add the nodev mount option alters filesystem mount behavior. Some applications or services may rely on character or block device files on non‑root partitions, and changing this option can cause service failures or boot problems. Policy directs filesystem/partition changes to be classified as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; Potential service disruption; Boot-time mount failures; Loss of access to device files on affected partitions; Requires system reboot for persistent effect
